title: Substack vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Drip email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your needs.
intro: Substack and Drip serve fundamentally different purposes in the email marketing landscape. Substack is a newsletter platform designed for writers to build and monetize direct subscriber relationships, while Drip specializes in e-commerce email automation with deep store integration. Your choice depends on whether you're focused on content monetization or e-commerce revenue optimization.

comparison_table.tool_a: Built-in audience discovery and paid subscription features
comparison_table.tool_b: Advanced e-commerce automation and revenue tracking

choose_a_if: You're a writer or content creator looking to monetize directly from readers
You want a completely free platform with no subscriber limits
You value built-in audience discovery and recommendation features
choose_b_if: You operate an e-commerce store and need revenue attribution tracking
You require SMS marketing and visual automation builders
You want advanced segmentation and onsite campaign capabilities

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—they target different markets. Substack excels for newsletter writers seeking direct monetization, while Drip is superior for e-commerce businesses needing sales tracking and automation. Choose based on your primary business model and needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Drip?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper for most users, offering a free tier with unlimited subscribers. Drip requires a minimum $39/month investment with no free option. However, Drip's revenue-based pricing could be favorable if your newsletter generates substantial paid subscriptions.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Drip and import it into Substack, though some data may not transfer perfectly. However, switching represents a significant platform change, as Drip's e-commerce automation features have no direct Substack equivalent. Evaluate your needs carefully before migrating.
